betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Inside the Reactor§f:
The interior of the Solid Fission Reactor is a hollow cube of casing blocks filled with functional components. Each component plays a specific role in the neutron economy and heat management of the reactor.

§6Key Components (NC Fission Multiblock)§f:
- §bFuel Cells§f: contain the LEU/HEU/MOX fuel. They produce heat and emit neutrons.
- §bHeat Sinks§f: passive coolers that absorb heat from adjacent fuel cells. There are 16+ variants, each with a different cooling material (water, iron, redstone, quartz, glowstone, lapis, gold, prismarine, slimeball, endstone, purpur, diamond, emerald, copper, tin, lead, boron, lithium, magnesium, manganese, aluminum, silver, fluorite, ...).
- §bModerators§f: blocks of graphite, beryllium, or heavy water. They slow fast neutrons to thermal energies, increasing fission probability. (FissionModeratorRecipes.java:18-20)
- §bReflectors§f: bounce escaping neutrons back into fuel. The Beryllium Reflector (meta 0) gives +50% return, the Lead Reflector (meta 1) gives +75% with high heat capacity. (CraftingRecipeHandler.java:231-232)
- §bIrradiators§f: process items using neutron flux (similar to QMD's Irradiator).
- §bNeutron Sources§f: kickstart the chain reaction when the reactor is cold.
- §bCell Ports§f: extract heat via fluid (water, NaK, helium). (CraftingRecipeHandler.java:236)
- §bVents§f: vent heat directly to air (less efficient than cell ports).

§6Key Reactor Stats§f:
- §bEfficiency§f: ratio of RF output to heat input. Higher with more moderators.
- §bHeat Generation§f: heat produced per tick, proportional to fuel enrichment and burn rate.
- §bCriticality§f: neutron flux multiplier. Above 1.0, the reaction is self-sustaining.
- §bFlux§f: actual neutron flow, the product of criticality and fuel enrichment.

§6Layout Strategy§f:
The arrangement of components determines the reactor's performance. Use the §bReactor Simulator§f item (NC:reactor_simulator) to preview stats before building. Common strategies:
- 5×5×5 large reactor: max efficiency, slow burn, easy to manage
- 3×3×3 compact reactor: high flux, fast burn, needs active cooling
- Asymmetric layouts: experimental designs that optimize specific stats

§6Direct Descendants of Solid Fission Controller (TaskRetrieval)§f:
The Solid Fission Controller's task is to assemble the reactor interior. The actual task item is the controller block itself, with all of its sub-components being separate quests (Heat Sink, Moderator Lines, Reflector).

§6Prerequisites§f:
Requires q30043 (Solid Fission Reactor) — the outer hull and controller block.

§eTip§f: Start with a 5×5×5 large reactor (max efficiency) for your first build. Once you understand the math, scale down to compact high-flux designs.

§eTip§f: For mid-game, use Heavy Water Moderators (made from Heavy Water Cells in an Ingot Former) — they give the best efficiency in the early game.

§cWarning§f: Don't place moderators next to reflectors without fuel between them — wasted moderation. Always use the Reactor Simulator before building an experimental layout.
